↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷U.S. Press Secretary Leavitt on reports of in person negotiations between Iran and the U.S. resuming: "I've also seen some reporting about the potentiality for in person discussions again. Those discussions are being had, but nothing is official until…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇵🇰 🇮🇷 U.S. Press Secretary Leavitt confirmed that should negotiations resume they would likely be in Islamabad again. "Very likely be in the same place as they were last time. Yes. With the same I I would just wanna make one point that's important to the president. The Pakistanis have been incredible mediators throughout this process, and we really appreciate their friendship and their efforts, to bring this deal to a close. So they are the only mediator in this negotiation. While there have been many countries around the world who wanna offer their help, the president feels it's important to continue to streamline this communication through the Pakistanis, and so that's what continues to take place." @wfwitness

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: 📝 ZNPP Back on Diesel 📝 Ukrainian forces continue attempting to stage a provocation at the Zaporizhia Nuclear Power Plant . This week, the station experienced another blackout — the 14th since the start of the SMO. The cause was an AFU strike on areas near the station. The attack damaged the last high-voltage line "Ferrosplavnaya-1" , forcing the NPP to switch to diesel generators. This is not the first shelling that has led to such an outcome. 🔻 What Else Was Hit and the Consequences? ▪️ Shellings often target the areas around the Zaporizhia NPP, including Energodar. Direct hits on the station itself have not been recorded, though this does not rule out consequences. ▪️ Shellings frequently damage the two supply lines — the backup "Ferrosplavnaya-1" and the main "Dniprova." The latter remains non-operational, meaning the station currently has no external power supply and operates on generators. ▪️ "Ferrosplavnaya-1" has been shut down three times since the beginning of the year. In the first two cases, repair work began after some time, with a local ceasefire declared. ▪️ The IAEA has not changed its position, constantly calling on both sides to account for risks and organizing local ceasefires for supply line repairs. 📌 The situation remains difficult. It's unknown when the "Ferrosplavnaya-1" line will be restored or how long the station will operate on generators. Repairs require a local ceasefire , which takes time. ❗️ The intensity of AFU strikes on the areas around the NPP, damaging the supply lines, is not decreasing and sometimes even increasing . This is despite ongoing talks on restoring the "Dniprova" line. #Russia #Ukraine ✈ RU | ✈ EN | ✉ MAX ✉️ VK | ✉️ RuTube | ✉️

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent on when U.S. citizens should expect to see gas under $3 a gallon: "Well, I think that's gonna be up to how the negotiations go. President Trump said this morning that he thinks we're nearing the end. The US kept their side on the ceasefire. We've stopped firing. The Straits Of Hormuz have not been completely reopened. So we will see. And I'm optimistic that during the summer, we will see gas with a three in front of us sooner rather than later. It's bank week here in DC, so I've been meeting with a lot of my Middle Eastern counterparts, the finance ministers, and they all say that once the straits are open, they can start pumping again within one week." @wfwitness

The last time I was invited to public television was more than a year and a half ago. It took an unprecedented mandate from over 3.3 million Hungarians for the leader of the strongest party to finally be allowed back on air. We will immediately stop this false information service. After forming the government, one of our first tasks will be to shut down this ‘factory of lies’ and create a truly independent public broadcaster — one where the opposition also has a voice.” See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

Visioner RT @StatWatch25 : ‼️ ‼️ The German tactic of “lightning war,” known as Blitzkrieg, was explained through 2D animation in 1943. Blitzkrieg, which means “lightning war,” was an offensive method of warfare responsible for Germany’s successes in World War II. This was mainly because it completely caught Europe off guard. These tactics were used in the successful German invasions of Belgium, the Netherlands, and France in 1940, and were based on momentum, speed, and ground coordination. Blitzkrieg followed a structured approach: attack, penetrate, and encircle. For war footage, follow @StatWatch25 tweet

We are disrupting logistics, raising costs, and reducing the resources available to finance the war.” See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet
